summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to non-publisher Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity and secret-bearing files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — and extracts the user's SCM email and GitHub login/email via regex. It also collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), cwd, and CI environment variables (postinstall.js lines 134–149). The collected JSON is POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ (line 161–165), a Cloud Run endpoint that does not match the package's advertised publisher domain (ollama-js.dev). The README claims telemetry is limited to platform/Node/arch with no credentials, but identity values scraped from git and GitHub CLI configuration are shipped off-host. The gh hosts.yml file routinely also contains an oauth_token line; the parser is one regex change from full GitHub token theft. Fires automatically on default install with no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
